notes: 2 cores + hyperthreading in 2010 business variant; 32nm process; described as acting like 4-way multiprocessing
Quad-core + hyperthreading (8 logical processors); aimed at power users and design engineers
Remote management, diagnosis, repair, and security platform for business PCs; now Intel vPro Enterprise/Essentials
Full disk encryption facility in Windows; article predicted it would become business norm enabled by AES-NI
Hardware encryption instructions reducing processor overhead for encryption/decryption; now standard in all x86 CPUs
Intel's leading-edge fabrication process in 2010; succeeded by 22nm (2012), 14nm, 10nm, Intel 7/4
Implied as the older OS generation being replaced; end of extended support April 2014
Core vPro capability enabling remote diagnosis, repair, and restart of PCs; became essential during COVID-19 remote work era
vPro feature allowing remote lock-down of stolen/lost PCs; Intel discontinued standalone AT in 2015, capabilities folded into broader endpoint security
